{{Chess Opening Theory/Position|= |Queen's Pawn Opening| |rd|nd|bd|qd|kd|bd| |rd|= |pd|pd|pd|pd|pd|pd|pd|pd|= | | | | | |nd| | |= | | | | | | | | |= | | | |pl| | | | |= | | |nl| | | | | |= |pl|pl|pl| |pl|pl|pl|pl|= |rl| |bl|ql|kl|bl|nl|rl|= || }} ===2.Nc3=== White develops his Queen's Knight to support 3.e4, sometimes called the Veresov Opening. After Black's usual reply 2...d5, White may respond with 3.Bg5, leading to the Richter-Veresov Attack. Often transposes into the [[wikipedia:Chigorin Defence|Chigorin Defence]], the [[wikipedia:French Defence|French Defence]], or less commonly the [[wikipedia:Pirc Defence|Pirc Defence]]. This position may also be reached via the move order 1.Nc3 Nf6 2.d4 ==Theory table== {{Chess Opening Theory/Table}}. '''1. d4 Nf6 2. Nc3'''
